hazard.summary: Same quota exhaustion STOP misclassification from cline #10139 independently propagated and fixed in OpenClaw without direct intervention — taxonomy adopted cross-repo
signals: SDK sleeps full duration before retrying — blocks OpenClaw failover layer
Anthropic/OpenAI SDKs check header first in shouldRetry() — skip retry immediately
Google/Ollama SDKs ignore header safely — no-op for non-affected providers

constraints: retry-after above threshold MUST inject x-should-retry: false before SDK retry logic runs
fix MUST be safe no-op for SDKs that do not check the header
short retry-after values MUST remain unaffected — transient throttling path preserved
knobs: x-should-retry: false header — injected into response before SDK shouldRetry() runs
retry-after-ms variant — parsed alongside standard retry-after for OpenAI compatibility
verification: Verify x-should-retry: false injected into response
Verify SDK skips retry and surfaces RateLimitError immediately
Verify OpenClaw failover layer receives error and routes correctly
notes:
This receipt documents cross-repo taxonomy propagation. jetd1 (OpenClaw contributor) independently implemented the same fix as cline PR #10140, explicitly citing cline/cline#10139 in commit messages. No direct involvement from Pitstop — the issue was clear enough to reference and generalize without prompting. This is the first corpus receipt documenting autonomous taxonomy adoption. The OpenClaw fix is more sophisticated than the cline fix: it parses retry-after-ms variant, makes the threshold configurable via environment variable, and explicitly handles the Google/Ollama SDK no-op case. Pattern: one well-documented issue becomes the reference implementation for a class of fixes across multiple repos.

Classification rules:
  retry-after > SDK_RETRY_MAX_WAIT_SECONDS → inject x-should-retry: false → STOP
  retry-after <= threshold → normal SDK retry → WAIT

Detection thresholds:
  60s default threshold distinguishes transient from quota exhaustion in practice
  threshold should be configurable per deployment context
